title: castela erecta subsp. texana
intro: Castela erecta subsp. texana, also known as the Texas Castorwood, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and distinctive, aromatic leaves. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its resin, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Castela erecta subsp. texana has been found to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as the ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a medicinal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and its resin is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate a range of health problems.
